title: Malty Silky Middle Eastern White Fish Mujadara
description: A lazy-sunday middle-eastern dish featuring white fish fillets, score-fat, and lacto-vegetarian-friendly ingredients, with a malty and silky texture, served in a mujadara form.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Drain and rinse the lentils, then cook them in 2 cups of water until tender.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and sauté the chopped onions until they are translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the malt syrup, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the cooked lentils to the pan and stir to combine with the onion mixture.
Season the white fish fillets with salt and black pepper.
Add the score-fat to the pan and cook the white fish fillets until they are cooked through.
Serve the white fish on top of the lentil mixture, with a dollop of plain yogurt on the side.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
